topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged, a sharp, unwelcome intrusion into the quiet hum of my laptop. My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I clicked it open, expecting a reminder about an assignment, but instead, I saw a picture of Mr. Harrison—my notoriously demanding history teacher—smiling broadly, standing in front of a campus landmark. A small, mocking caption accompanied the photo, referencing a recent class discussion we’d had. A wave of pure irritation washed over me, hot and prickly. I slammed the laptop shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the empty room. I wanted to shove the device into the drawer, to erase the evidence of this unwelcome surveillance. Every muscle in my jaw tightened.

[story 2]

I stared at my phone screen, the glass reflecting back my own strained expression. It was his profile picture, cropped slightly, looking far too relaxed for the stern figure I remembered from the classroom. A slow burn started in my chest, a simmering resentment that made my palms sweat. I scrolled through his recent posts—pictures of weekend activities, casual gatherings—and felt a surge of profound annoyance. Why was he broadcasting this? It felt like an invasion, a deliberate showing off that grated on my nerves. I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to steady my breathing, but it hitched in my throat. I couldn't bring myself to block him immediately; instead, I just kept staring, letting the tension build until my vision felt slightly blurred at the edges.

[story 3]

The email arrived just as I was trying to focus on calculus. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. The message was innocuous enough—a simple question about a book we were assigned—but the context made my skin crawl. He had tagged me in a story about a university lecture, a public display of his attention. A distinct feeling of being watched, judged, intensified, making the air in my small dorm room feel suddenly thick and suffocating. I picked up my pen, but my hand trembled so violently I had to pause. I reread the message three times, the words seeming to twist into something sharp and accusatory in my mind. I shoved the phone under my pillow, desperately trying to quell the rising tide of simmering displeasure.
